page: Amazon Web Services r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across multi-core CPU tasks and memory operations. The instance achieves average ratings in stress-ng multi-core performance, Passmark CPU tests, and memory bandwidth tests, with uncached read speeds of 50.08 GB/sec and write speeds of 35.76 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests falling into the bottom 25% tier. Large-model LLM inference is poor, with the 7B model achieving under 10 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5.2xlarge offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive single-core CPU performance. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and web serving, as demonstrated by its average performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. It is less suitable for CPU-heavy compilation, ray tracing, or machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System, this x86_64 instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and featuring a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU capabilities and memory bandwidth, though single-core processing and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the lower tier. This hardware profile makes the r5.2xlarge qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications that do not demand high single-thread compute. It is best suited for database operations, in-memory caching like Redis, and general web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for memory-intensive workloads like databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio, designed for database operations and in-memory caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Tradeoffs include weaker single-core compute and no local storage
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and web serving
